steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage contrasts two categories: established literary forms like the short story that are "recognizable as such even as they evolve," versus rarer "intergeneric" forms that are "unclassifiable." The blank describes the first category — forms that hold clear, conventional identities.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'equivocal': means ambiguous or uncertain. The recognizable forms are the opposite of uncertain — eliminate. ✗
  • Choice B — 'orthodox': means following established conventions and traditional forms. Directly describes literary forms that maintain recognizable identities. ✓
  • Choice C — 'intermediate': suggests a transitional position between two things. Nothing in the passage frames these forms as in-between anything — eliminate. ✗
  • Choice D — 'neutral': means neither one thing nor another. Doesn't capture the idea of following recognized conventions — eliminate. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
